student_name: João Guilherme da Costa e Silva
hub_subtitle: Business English técnico para reuniões com fornecedores internacionais, normas e entrevistas

lesson.menu_title: Holding Your Ground -- The Supplier Approval Meeting
lesson.menu_desc: O relê reprovou no FAT e o fornecedor chama de "minor deviation": voz passiva avançada, causativo have something done e a reunião de hold point -- 40 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- Holding Your Ground
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- João Guilherme | Lesson 6 -- Holding Your Ground

listenings.text: João, it is Camille. I said I would call you before Friday, and I would rather you heard this from me than read it in a report. We have the root cause. A reference instrument in our calibration laboratory had drifted, and it had not been recalibrated on schedule. The drift was identified on Tuesday, and every unit tested against that instrument since April is now being reviewed. Yours was not the only one. The corrective action plan will be sent to you tomorrow, the relay will be retested on Thursday, and the certificate will be issued before the retest, not after it. Now the part I have to ask, and you are free to say no. If the retest passes, I would like the inspection release on Friday and not the following week, because the shipment is booked and the rework has already cost us three days. And one more thing. On Monday I called it a minor deviation. I should not have. If our positions had been reversed, I would not have signed it off either, and I would have held my ground exactly the way you did.

Before you raise that non-conformance report, three things, because engineers get these wrong more often than anybody else. First. The passive is not cold English and it is not weak English. It is the only register that survives a disagreement. If you write, your technician calibrated the relay badly, you have written an accusation, and an accusation can be denied, defended and argued about for three weeks. If you write, the relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test, you have written a finding. The person is out of the sentence and the fact is in it, and a finding does not get argued with. It gets closed. Every standard on your shelf is written this way, and there is a reason. Second. Do not agree a corrective action before the root cause has been identified. The relay reading eleven milliseconds is a symptom. The reason it read eleven is the root cause, and until somebody has found it, whatever you agree on will fail a second time, at your cost and on your schedule. Ask for the root cause first. Always. Third, and this is the one that costs money. Never let the supplier choose the word. A deviation is approved before the work is done. Anything found after the work is a non-conformance, and it has to be corrected or formally waived. The moment you accept the word deviation for something that was found, not agreed, you have accepted the rework, and you will pay for it. State the finding, ask for the cause, name the date, and let them tell you how they will fix it. That is the whole meeting.

patterns.title: Four structures that carry an approval meeting
cards: You state a finding, not a person
The register of every inspection report. Nobody is accused, and nothing can be denied. "The relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test."
It fixes the gap in time: at the moment of the test, the document did not exist. "The calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test."
The obligation belongs to the process
The requirement comes from the procedure, not from you, so it cannot be taken personally. "A corrective action must be implemented before the retest."
You require the work; they perform it
The object comes first, the participle second. Your demand, their hands, their cost. "We need to have the report resubmitted by Friday."

patterns.text: The passive is not decoration and it is not politeness. It is what lets you say something serious about a company without saying anything about a person. Use it for the finding; use the causative for the demand.

reading.rtitle: Inspection Report -- Factory Acceptance Test, Safety Relay SR-2140 (supplier, extract)
paras: The functional test was carried out on 14 July in the presence of the client's representative. The safety relay was found to be out of tolerance: a response time of eleven milliseconds was recorded against a specified range of five to seven. The test was witnessed and countersigned, a hold point was triggered, and no inspection release has been issued for the unit.

It should be noted that the calibration certificate for the reference instrument had not been issued at the time of the test. The failure is believed to have been caused by a drift in that instrument, although the root cause has not yet been formally identified. The calibration itself was performed by an approved subcontractor, and traceability for the batch is being reviewed. In the view of the supplier, the departure from the specification is minor and does not affect the safety function of the unit.

It is therefore requested that the finding be recorded as a deviation and that a waiver be granted, so that the unit can be shipped on the original date. Should this not be accepted, the rework will be carried out at the supplier's cost, the unit will be retested, and a revised report will be resubmitted through the approval workflow. Sign-off would then be requested at the earliest opportunity.
reading.source: Supplier Quality Department -- Inspection Report, extract (fictional, written for João's package)

reading.text: Notice the collocations: carry out a test, issue a certificate, trigger a hold point, identify the root cause, grant a waiver, resubmit a report, request sign-off.

gist.prompt: What is the report actually asking for?
choices: It reports that the safety relay failed the functional test and that a hold point was triggered.
It asks for the failure to be reclassified as a deviation and waived, so that the unit can ship on the original date without rework.
It confirms that the root cause has been identified and that a corrective action is already in place.
It informs the client that the rework has been completed and that sign-off can now be granted.

items: The calibration certificate was valid at the time of the functional test.
f
"...the calibration certificate for the reference instrument had not been issued at the time of the test."
An inspection release has been issued for the relay.
f
"...a hold point was triggered, and no inspection release has been issued for the unit."
The report confirms that the root cause has been identified.
f
It says the failure "is believed to have been caused by a drift" -- but "the root cause has not yet been formally identified". Believed is not identified.
The supplier says the rework would be done at its own cost if the waiver is refused.
t
"Should this not be accepted, the rework will be carried out at the supplier's cost..."
The supplier treats the failure as a serious non-conformance.
f
"In the view of the supplier, the departure from the specification is minor..." -- and it asks for the finding to be recorded as a deviation.

items.situation: "So, what exactly is the problem?" Open the meeting with the finding. No names.
tips: The relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test: eleven milliseconds against a specified five to seven.
The test was witnessed, a hold point was triggered, and no inspection release has been issued.

items.situation: "The certificate was issued the following Monday." Say why that does not solve it.
tips: The calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test, so it does not cover the test.
The unit will have to be retested with a valid certificate in place.

items.situation: "It is a minor deviation. The unit works." Refuse the word, and give the criterion.
tips: A deviation is approved before the work. This was found after it, on a safety relay.
I am afraid that is a non-conformance, and it has to be corrected or formally waived.

items.situation: "The calibration was performed by our subcontractor, not by us." Put the responsibility back.
tips: To some extent, I understand your position. However, our contract is with you.
The root cause still has to be identified on your side, and the corrective action has to come from you.

items.situation: "What do you need from us before you release the unit?" Three things, with a date.
tips: The root cause, a corrective action plan, and the relay retested with a valid certificate, by Friday.
We need to have the report resubmitted through the approval workflow before any sign-off is granted.

items.situation: "Can you at least sign it off provisionally?" Say no without closing the door.
tips: I am afraid sign-off cannot be granted while the hold point remains open.
As soon as the retest passes and the certificate is on file, the inspection release can be issued the same day.

bank.label: Stating the finding
items: The relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test.
A response time of eleven milliseconds was recorded against a specified range of five to seven.
The calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test.

bank.label: Hedging before the hard fact
items: It would appear that the deviation was caused by the calibration drift.
To some extent, I understand your position. However, our contract is with you.
I am afraid that is a non-conformance, and not a deviation.

bank.label: Making the demand
items: A corrective action must be implemented before the retest.
We need to have the report resubmitted through the approval workflow by Friday.
We will have the relay retested with a valid certificate in place.

bank.label: Holding the gate
items: Sign-off cannot be granted while the hold point remains open.
I would like to raise a concern about the traceability of that batch.
As soon as the retest passes, the inspection release can be issued the same day.
